Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Continued To Oct-15-15 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 210

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Continued To Nov-09-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to CROSS-DEFENDANTS THE HOME DEPOT AND HELMSMAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT, the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff does not show that Defendant's construction of the settlement agreement is clearly erroneous. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning that the instrument is reasonably susceptible. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading."). The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support the claim and less particularity is required when the defendant should possess full information regarding the dispute. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT

Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Answer To Complt
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE and MICHAEL COFFIELD'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer To Complaint, And Cross Complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and the proposed amendments are transactionally related and do not change the tenor of the case. In addition, there is no prejudice to The Home Depot/Helmsman Management Services. The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANT RAYMOND JETER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant provides no conflicts of law analysis and fails to demonstrate that Georgia law applies. See Kearney v. Salomon Smith Barney, Inc. (2006) 39 Cal.4th 95, 107-108. The Court signed the ORDER submitted by email. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
